commit.message:
Fix rework deadlock: re-enqueue items after build-blocked or no-output

After build-blocked rework or no FILE: blocks output, rework items were
consumed from ReworkQueue but never re-enqueued. This caused the agent to
loop forever with CurrentPrNumber set but nothing to do - could never reach
max rework cycles for force-approval, blocking the entire task pipeline.

Now re-enqueue items after both failure paths so retry/force-approval works.

Fix SYSLIB1045 code fixer generating duplicate MyRegex names across partial class declarations (#124698)

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/77409

## Problem

When the SYSLIB1045 `BatchFixer` applies multiple code fixes
concurrently to separate partial class declarations, each fix
independently checks existing members via the semantic model. Since all
fixers see the **original** compilation (before any fix is applied),
they all conclude `MyRegex` is available and generate duplicate property
names, causing CS0756.

## Fix

In `UpgradeToGeneratedRegexCodeFixer.CreateGeneratedRegexProperty`,
after finding the first available name via the semantic model, the new
`CountPrecedingRegexCallSites` helper scans all
`DeclaringSyntaxReferences` of the containing type for Regex
constructor/static-method call sites that would also generate new
property names. These are sorted deterministically (by file path, then
span position). The current node's index in that sorted list determines
how many names to skip, giving each concurrent fixer a unique name.

## Test

Added `CodeFixerGeneratesUniqueNamesAcrossPartialClassDeclarations` —
two `new Regex(...)` calls in separate partial class declarations of the
same type, verifying `MyRegex` and `MyRegex1` are generated. Verified
the test fails without the fix (both declarations produce `MyRegex`).

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix two concurrency bugs in Encoding::start() and ResourceManager::authzQueue_

Encoding::start() had a TOCTOU race: the running_ guard check and the
thread/worker startup were in separate lock scopes. Two concurrent callers
could both pass the guard, then both execute the startup path serially.
The second would call std::thread::operator= on a joinable readThread_,
which calls std::terminate(). Fixed by combining both lock scopes into one.

ResourceManager::authzQueue_ was accessed from signatureCheckQueue_'s
worker thread (handleVerifiedMessage) and from the destructor without
holding any mutex, while rebuildAuthorizationQueue() can replace it from
any thread. std::shared_ptr assignment is not atomic, so concurrent read
and write is undefined behaviour. Fixed by adding authzQueueMutex_ to
guard all post-construction accesses. The destructor now also stops
signatureCheckQueue_ before taking authzQueue_ so the feeder is drained
before the queue it feeds is removed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
PRD: Phase 2 — Wire generate-playbook into the build (#124)

* chore: PRD: Phase 2 — Wire generate-playbook into the build (closes #118)

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on PR #124

- _discover_overlay_variables: handle "var | default(false)" pattern
  so laptop overlay variable is correctly discovered
- _generate_host_vars_json_template: emit quoted dict keys for valid
  Jinja2 ({"bluetooth": bluetooth} not {bluetooth: bluetooth})
- write_playbook: deep copy _SECTION_DEFINITIONS to prevent mutation
  across multiple calls
- write_playbook: use profile-defined tags from role_cond.tags instead
  of deriving from role name (preserves [fonts] tag on nerd-fonts)
- write_playbook: emit double-quoted tags for Makefile grep compat
- Remove duplicate _cmd_generate_playbook function and duplicate
  argparse registration
- Update generate-playbook tests for file-write mode
- Regenerate play.yml with fixed generator

* fix: correct Jinja2 combine() pattern in host_vars_json template

Move the 'if cond else {}' inside combine() to prevent data loss
when later overlay variables are not defined. The pattern:
  | combine({...}) if cond else {}
could discard all previously accumulated vars when 'cond' is False.
The correct pattern is:
  | combine({...} if cond else {})

Fix applied in both _generate_host_vars_json_template() and play.yml.

fix(kernel): resolve concurrency races in BudgetPool and parallel tool execution

- BudgetPool.Preempt: upgrade RLock to write Lock for safe snapshot+mutate
- BudgetPool.TryReserve: hold RLock for full duration including MaxTokens/MaxSteps reads
- Session: add thread-safe metadata accessors (Set/Get/Delete/Ensure/Copy/BatchMetadata)
- SyncIO: new wrapper serializing IO calls during parallel tool execution

commit.message:
Restrict named capture group names to ASCII letters/digits (#130)

Match java.util.regex.Pattern rules: first character must be an ASCII
letter (A-Z, a-z), subsequent characters must be ASCII letters or
digits (A-Z, a-z, 0-9). Previously SafeRE was too lenient, accepting
underscores, Unicode letters/digits, and connector punctuation, and
also allowing names to start with digits.

* perf(nvim): only load view once per buffer using view_activated flag

The previous implementation called loadview on every BufWinEnter,
causing repeated disk reads each time you switched between windows.
Now uses a per-buffer view_activated flag (AstroNvim-style) so:
- loadview runs only once per buffer, on first entry
- filetype check ensures treesitter has set up folding before restoring
- mkview is only called when the view has been activated, avoiding
  overwriting views for buffers that were never loaded
- BufWritePost and WinLeave added as additional save triggers

Implements the architecture & design foundation for migrating from
Nix + Home Manager to chezmoi + mise. All files are additive —
existing Nix configuration is untouched (Phase 1: non-destructive).

New files:
- .chezmoi.toml.tmpl: chezmoi config template with machine/user detection
- .chezmoiignore: per-machine file exclusion with WSL/enterprise conditionals
- .chezmoiexternal.toml: external git repos (zsh plugins, tpm)
- .chezmoidata/packages.yaml: full tool audit (Tier 1–4 classification)
- .chezmoidata/users.yaml: user profiles for naroslife, enterpriseuser
- .mise.toml: global tool versions with ubi/pipx/cargo backends + task runner
- bootstrap.sh: new entry point (online/offline/no-mise modes)
- home/dot_zshrc.tmpl: Zsh config with kill-switch-aware aliases
- home/dot_gitconfig.tmpl: per-user git config via chezmoi template
- home/dot_ssh/config.tmpl: SSH config with WSL/enterprise conditionals
- home/dot_config/{starship,atuin,bottom,ripgrep}: tool configs
- home/run_onchange_install-zsh-plugins.sh.tmpl: plugin clone hook
- scripts/install-apt-deps.sh: Tier 1/3/4 apt dependency installer
- scripts/install-zsh-plugins.sh: git clone Zsh plugins + tpm
- scripts/install-fonts.sh: Nerd Fonts downloader
- scripts/kill-switch.sh: revert all modern CLI aliases (sourceable)
- docs/adr/ADR-001-chezmoi-mise-migration.md: architecture decision record

Updated:
- ARCHITECTURE.md: documents target + legacy architecture, migration status

commit.message:
fix(acp-log): suppress consecutive chunk frames via envelope.skip

Consecutive agent_message_chunk / agent_thought_chunk notifications
flood the ACP debug log. Only the first frame of each consecutive
same-kind + same-direction run is logged now.

Design:
- acpLogEnvelope gains updateKind (set by filterNotifySessionUpdateBody)
  and skip bool (set by the sink before deciding to log)
- acpLastRecord struct holds the last-recorded updateKind+dir pair
- defaultACPProcessLogSink is a pointer receiver with a mutex and a
  lastRecord field
- applyChunkFilter(dir, *envelope) is a focused helper: if the envelope
  is a chunk kind and matches lastRecord, it sets skip=true; otherwise
  it updates lastRecord (and resets it for non-chunk frames)
- Frame() builds the envelope, calls applyChunkFilter, returns early if
  envelope.skip is true
- filterACPBody / filterNotifySessionUpdateBody return ([]byte, string)
  to surface updateKind without a second JSON parse

📝 docs: apply N1-N7 decisions, fix remaining D-series divergences

- Fix angle bracket leaks: Vec<LineItem> → Vec[LineItem], Option<Self.Item> → Option[Self.Item], FuncCall<f> → FuncCall[f] (D8)
- Fix refinement if → when in all type aliases (D6/D10)
- Fix EBNF error set syntax: "!" "[" Types "]" → "!" TypeExpr { "|" TypeExpr } (D11)
- Fix spec clause separator: example "..." => … → example "...": … (N5)
- Fix where multi-bound: remove T: Eq + Hash, add N4 note (N4)
- Fix EBNF Capability → Trait in Program production (D2)
- Add N1–N7 decisions to DESIGN.md decision table

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace dead Clearbit logos with Google favicons + update CSP for thumbnails

- ainews.js: Replace logo.clearbit.com (dead) with google.com/s2/favicons?sz=64
- staticwebapp.config.json: Simplify img-src to allow https: (article thumbnails
  come from arbitrary CDNs that can't be individually whitelisted)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: extract thumbnails from RSS content HTML and og:image fallback

- Add extract_image_from_html() to find images in entry.content and summary HTML
- Add fetch_og_image() to fetch og:image meta tag from article pages
- Add backfill_og_images() with parallel fetching (8 workers) as final fallback
- Check entry.links for image types
- Should improve thumbnail coverage from ~10% to ~80%+

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
